author: barnabasbusa
content:
it’s really not about that. 

CLs don’t support RLP really…  Carrying opaque RLP blobs is very different from having a full RLP codec that can serialize/deserialize structured Engine API types like ForkchoiceStateV1

author: barnabasbusa
content: The major benefit of ssz is the schema & Merkleization support

author: barnabasbusa
content: I have no skin in the game. I have no benefit in “ssz supremacy”, I’m not a CL dev, I couldn’t care less if it’s rlp or ssz if rlp would be a good alternative I’d have proposed that. But it’s not. When I look at a problem that we trying to solve I’m trying to look at the whole flow from EL to CL

author: barnabasbusa
content: Trying to see how we can reduce the total time spent on duplicating work basically

author: barnabasbusa
content: Having native ssz support on the EL side would definitely help making this easier and faster , even if it means significant changes required for the ELs

author: potuz
content:
we could suppor RLP if it were necessary: but the point is that the conversion to SSZ will have to be like that because SSZ is on our state transition function, so the argument is pretty simple, there are only two options: RLP and SSZ, any other option is stupid because it would require two conversions. 

So either we have the EL send the RLP and the CL converts it to SSZ or we have the EL convert to SSZ and send it. These are reallistically the only two options.

author: barnabasbusa
content: Yeah it makes no sense to do 2 conversation when we could do just 1

author: potuz
content: now why the EL converting to SSL is better than the option, I can think of a few but one of them is existing tooling that proves against something, monitoring tooling that already relies on SSZ, etc. There is nothing that relies on RLP outside of the EL, and the engine is something that was only created after the CL so all of the tooling around monitoring the engine is SSZ based, not RLP

author: potuz
content: you could argue for RLP on eth_* APIs etc. those I don't care, but engine itself affects mostly CL tooling and not EL tooling.

author: _shemnon
content: I bring this up preemtpively because ACDE has had famous arguments over SSZ with some people notoriously resistant.  If there was a good reason like "SSZ lets you elide large signatures from transactions and preserve the TX hash" (but a reason specific to the  engine API) that would set a clear advantage to one over the other. If the argument is supremacy and "why not" then RLP deserves consideration.

author: potuz
content: in this particular case it honestly doesn't need to be speced, it's optional and not a fork

author: potuz
content: if any client implements it we'll take it

author: barnabasbusa
content: without spec nobody will implement it

author: potuz
content: clients that don't implement it will be dropped as non-performant

author: barnabasbusa
content: This optional performance improvement is much needed tho imo

author: potuz
content: cc <@687974325072035882> that may have something different to say about this

author: barnabasbusa
content: Having a single client implementation that works with every CL without a spec is a nice vibe project but won’t be a well supported solution. With a spec we can clearly point to a specific structure we expect to have both from EL and CLs

author: siladu
content:
For more context: benchmarks for JSON getBlobs with 21, 72, 128 blobs (including network, excluding CL side)
https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1443978877440626809/1464047453438349426

Not sure exactly when performance becomes an issue but I don't think it's imminent so happy to see it is opt-in and clients can move at their own pace 👍
